intro: Clidemia juruensis, also known as Clidemia, is a plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Clidemia juruensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Clidemia is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
